steps.title: Identify the boundary type needed
steps.content: The sentence has two independent clauses joined at the underlined point: “Spacewar! challenged players to [achieve something]” and “Before Spacewar!’s development, gaming was [something].” Two independent clauses cannot be joined with only a comma or a bare conjunction that doesn’t fit — they need a period, semicolon, or comma + coordinating conjunction.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices, confirm the correct one
steps.content:
• Choice A — players, before: joins two independent clauses with only a comma — a comma splice. ✗
  • Choice B — players and before: “and” cannot link the noun “players” to the preposition “before”; the result is ungrammatical. ✗
  • Choice C — players. Before: a period ends the first independent clause, and “Before” starts a new sentence correctly. ✓
  • Choice D — players before: joins two independent clauses with no punctuation at all — a fused (run-on) sentence. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
